era: 2003-2009. Post-Metal / Post-Hardcore. E=8 -- accumulated textural weight, architectural heaviness. Capitol Hill, Seattle. Suicide Squeeze Records.
associated_musicians: Russian Circles (Sargent House -- same label as Helms Alee, S36-331)

theory_nodes.concept: Brian Cook throughline: Botch (332) to These Arms Are Snakes (333) to Russian Circles to Sargent House (Helms Alee, 331). Three consecutive degrees connected by one bassist.
theory_nodes.description: Most connected bassist in the engine's metal arc. Three consecutive degrees via direct membership and label ecosystem.

theory_nodes.concept: Post-metal architecture: long-form structures, extreme dynamic range, textural processing, compositional logic closer to modern classical than rock. These Arms Are Snakes add hardcore urgency to standard post-metal formula.
theory_nodes.description: Capitol Hill post-metal: atmospheric weight plus aggressive urgency.

theory_nodes.concept: Metal Summit opening: degree 333 establishes architectural complexity as the first principle of the four-degree maximum-energy arc. E=8, 9, 9, 9 through 336.
theory_nodes.description: Sustained maximum energy arc begins here.

cross_references.relationship: Brian Cook played bass in both bands. Botch dissolved 2002, Cook joined Snakes immediately. The mathcore tradition (Botch) feeds the post-metal tradition (Snakes) via the same bassist.
Helms Alee on Sargent House; Russian Circles (Brian Cook's other band) also on Sargent House. Indirect label connection.
Metal Summit continues. E=8 to E=9. Post-metal architecture to sludge primogeniture.
E=8 + E=8 = E=16. Architecture of maximum weather vs. architecture of maximum metal. Both are full-spectrum, full-intensity events.
